content.full_text:
AMERICAN SAVINGS
BANK
A
=i
MARGEFY HINTEBMAN
Paralegal
Direct Dial O14) 252-4773
F6x ?14) 252-4799
March 15, 1991
Ms. Lynda Cassady
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 958.14
Re: Merger ol Affliated PACS
American Savings Bank PAC FPPC #891G6
American Savings Bank Employee PAC FPPC #891082
Dear Ms. Cassady:
I spoke with you several months ago about merging the two referenced Political Action
Committees. ln orderto deplete the balance of funds of PAC FPPC #891082, contributions have
been made to candidates, etc. and no member contributions have been received since May
1990. PAC FPPC #891056 is the Associatlon's PAC that has been receiving contributions lrom
its members. Because dual reporting is duplicative and time-consuming, it would be to our
advantage as well as yours to merge the two PACS.
According to our telephone conversation, I understand we may transfer the entire balance oi one
PAC to the other by liling a Form 415 with a Form 420. The'transfer out" of the balance of PAC
#891082 will be shown on Schedule E and the "transfer in" to Pac #891056 will be shown on
Schedule G. Form 420 will show a "0" balance.
lf you conrirm that the procedure described above is correct, please sign the enclosed copy of
this letter and return it to me in the enclosed self-addressed envelope. ll inconect, please call me
al (714\ 252-4773 and discuss the correct procedure with me.
I appreciate you assistance in this matter.
Sincerely,
Margery Hintefman
17877 von Karman. P.0. Box 19689, lryine, California S27139689 17141 252 4709


Lynda Cassady
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 15, 1991
page 2
CONFIRMED
Name
Enclosure
MH
Date
cc: George R. Bravante, Jr., Treasurer (w/o enc.)
William D. Hoel, Chairman (w/o enc.)

sections.question_synthetic: What is the correct procedure for merging two affiliated Political Action Committees (PACs) and transferring the fund balance from one PAC to another?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from a bank seeking FPPC guidance, not an FPPC advice letter. The document shows 'CONFIRMED' signature line on page 2, suggesting it may have been signed by Ms. Cassady, but contains no formal FPPC response text. No standard question/conclusion sections present. Treated as correspondence rather than formal advice letter due to lack of FPPC-authored response content.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: What is the correct procedure for merging two affiliated Political Action Committees (PACs) and transferring the fund balance from one PAC to another?

embedding.summary: American Savings Bank's paralegal seeks confirmation from the FPPC regarding the proper procedure for merging two affiliated PACs (FPPC #891056 and #891082) by transferring the entire balance using Forms 415 and 420 with appropriate schedule entries.
